reverb-music-gear-listings.longDescription:
## Reverb Music Gear Listings API

Access real-time listing data from Reverb.com — the world's largest marketplace for musical instruments and audio equipment.

### Features
- Search guitars, basses, amps, effects pedals, synths, drums, DJ gear & studio equipment
- Returns listing price, condition (mint/excellent/good/fair), seller rating, shipping info
- Direct listing URLs for each result
- Up to 50 results per search, structured JSON

### Built For
Developers building gear price comparison engines, deal-finding bots, resale arbitrage tools, and music gear aggregators.

### Example Use
Query `?q=fender stratocaster&minPrice=500&maxPrice=1500` → returns matching listings with prices, conditions, and seller details.

**Free tier**: 50 requests/month.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
curl -X GET 'https://reverb-music-gear-listings.p.rapidapi.com/reverb/search?query=fender+stratocaster&limit=10' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: reverb-music-gear-listings.p.rapidapi.com'
```

### Python
```python
import requests

url = 'https://reverb-music-gear-listings.p.rapidapi.com/reverb/search'
params = {'query': 'fender stratocaster', 'limit': '10'}
headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'reverb-music-gear-listings.p.rapidapi.com'
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params)
listings = response.json()
for item in listings.get('results', []):
    print(f"{item['title']} - ${item['price']} ({item['condition']})")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const options = {
  method: 'GET',
  headers: {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'reverb-music-gear-listings.p.rapidapi.com'
  }
};

fetch('https://reverb-music-gear-listings.p.rapidapi.com/reverb/search?query=fender+stratocaster&limit=10', options)
  .then(res => res.json())
  .then(data => console.log(data.results));
```

### Pricing & Plans
The **free plan** (25 calls/month) lets you test gear searches, validate the response format, and prototype your listing aggregator. Upgrade to a paid plan for production-volume deal monitoring, price tracking, and inventory workflows.

poshmark-fashion-resale.shortDescription: Search Poshmark for secondhand fashion — clothing, shoes, bags & accessories. Filter by brand, category, price. Returns sizes, conditions, seller ratings, and photos.
poshmark-fashion-resale.longDescription:
## Poshmark Fashion Resale API

Search Poshmark — the largest social marketplace for secondhand fashion in the US. Get structured JSON data for clothing, shoes, handbags, jewelry, accessories, and home goods with pricing, sizes, conditions, seller ratings, and photos.

### Features
- **Fashion categories**: Women's, men's, kids' clothing, shoes, handbags, jewelry, accessories, beauty, and home goods
- **Brand coverage**: Designer (Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Chanel) to everyday brands (Nike, Lululemon, Zara) — 9,000+ brands
- **Listing details**: Title, price, original price, size, condition, brand, color, photos
- **Seller data**: Username, rating, number of sales, closet size, response time
- **Flexible search**: Keyword search with optional limit parameter

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/poshmark/search` | Search Poshmark listings by keyword |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Search term (brand, item type, keywords)
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "query": "louis vuitton neverfull",
  "totalResults": 156,
  "results": [
    {
      "title": "Louis Vuitton Neverfull MM Monogram",
      "price": 1250.00,
      "originalPrice": 2030.00,
      "brand": "Louis Vuitton",
      "size": "OS",
      "condition": "Good",
      "color": "Brown",
      "seller": "luxurycloset_nyc",
      "sellerRating": 4.9,
      "likes": 47,
      "imageUrl": "https://di2ponv0v5otw.cloudfront.net/...",
      "listingUrl": "https://poshmark.com/listing/...",
      "category": "Bags"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Resale arbitrage tools**: Find underpriced designer items on Poshmark to flip on eBay, Mercari, or Depop
- **Fashion price trackers**: Monitor secondhand prices for specific brands or items over time
- **Inventory sourcing bots**: Automatically discover new listings matching your sourcing criteria
- **Cross-marketplace aggregators**: Combine Poshmark with Grailed, Depop, and ThredUp in one search
- **Market research dashboards**: Analyze resale value trends for designer brands and categories
- **Closet management apps**: Help sellers price items competitively by showing comparable listings

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | React-rendered HTML, requires headless browser | Clean JSON with every field extracted |
| **Anti-bot** | Aggressive CAPTCHA and IP blocking | No bot detection to deal with |
| **Auth** | No public API available | Simple API key authentication |
| **Maintenance** | Poshmark updates break scrapers regularly | We handle all site changes |
| **Development time** | 1-2 weeks for headless browser scraper | 5 minutes to first API call |
| **Cost** | Proxy costs + headless browser infra | One API subscription |

Poshmark aggressively blocks scrapers and has no public API. Their site requires JavaScript rendering and uses anti-bot technology. This API handles all of that complexity.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
curl -G "https://poshmark-fashion-resale.p.rapidapi.com/poshmark/search" \
  -d "query=louis+vuitton+neverfull" -d "limit=10" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: poshmark-fashion-resale.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
    "X-RapidAPI-Host": "poshmark-fashion-resale.p.rapidapi.com"
}

# Search for designer bags
response = requests.get(
    "https://poshmark-fashion-resale.p.rapidapi.com/poshmark/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "chanel classic flap", "limit": 10}
)
for item in response.json().get("results", []):
    discount = round((1 - item["price"] / item["originalPrice"]) * 100) if item.get("originalPrice") else 0
    print(f"{item['title']} - ${item['price']} ({discount}% off retail)")
    print(f"  Brand: {item['brand']} | Condition: {item['condition']} | Likes: {item['likes']}")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
  "X-RapidAPI-Host": "poshmark-fashion-resale.p.rapidapi.com"
};

const res = await fetch(
  "https://poshmark-fashion-resale.p.rapidapi.com/poshmark/search?query=lululemon+align&limit=10",
  { headers }
);
const data = await res.json();
data.results.forEach(item => {
  console.log(`${item.title} — $${item.price} (was $${item.originalPrice})`);
  console.log(`  Size: ${item.size} | Condition: ${item.condition} | Seller: ${item.seller}`);
});
```

### Pricing & Plans
The **free plan** (50 calls/month) lets you test fashion searches, validate the JSON schema, and prototype your resale tool. Upgrade to a paid plan for production-volume inventory sourcing, price tracking, and cross-marketplace arbitrage workflows.

craigslist-classifieds-search.longDescription:
## Craigslist Classifieds Search API

Search Craigslist — the original classifieds platform covering 700+ cities across 70+ countries. Find housing, jobs, for-sale items, services, gigs, and community posts. Get structured JSON with pricing, locations, GPS coordinates, posting dates, and category data.

### Features
- **All categories**: For sale, housing (apartments, rooms, sublets), jobs, services, gigs, community, and resumes
- **700+ cities**: Every US metro area plus major international cities
- **Location data**: City, neighborhood, GPS coordinates (latitude/longitude) for map-based applications
- **Listing details**: Title, price, location, posting date, description, images, and direct URL
- **Category filtering**: Search within specific sections (apa, car, rea, etc.) or across all categories
- **Real-time data**: Craigslist posts in real time — new listings appear within minutes

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/craigslist/search` | Search Craigslist listings by keyword and city |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Search term (item, job title, housing type, etc.)
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "query": "studio apartment",
  "totalResults": 89,
  "results": [
    {
      "title": "Sunny Studio Apt in Mission District - Laundry/Parking",
      "price": 1850.00,
      "location": "Mission District, SF",
      "city": "sfbay",
      "category": "apa",
      "postedDate": "2026-03-30T09:15:00Z",
      "latitude": 37.7599,
      "longitude": -122.4148,
      "imageUrl": "https://images.craigslist.org/...",
      "url": "https://sfbay.craigslist.org/sfc/apa/...",
      "attributes": {
        "sqft": 450,
        "bedrooms": 0,
        "laundry": "in-building",
        "parking": "street"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Rental trackers**: Monitor apartment listings and alert when new units match criteria (price, neighborhood, size)
- **Job aggregators**: Combine Craigslist job posts with Indeed, LinkedIn, and Glassdoor data
- **Deal-finding bots**: Surface underpriced for-sale items before they sell — electronics, furniture, vehicles
- **Real estate research**: Track rental pricing trends across neighborhoods and metro areas
- **Local market analytics**: Analyze posting volume, pricing, and demand by city and category
- **Moving planning tools**: Compare cost of living (rent, furniture, services) across cities

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | Minimal HTML but aggressive anti-bot (IP blocks) | Clean JSON with every field extracted |
| **Rate limits** | Craigslist bans IPs aggressively for scraping | Standard API rate limits, no bans |
| **Proxy costs** | Need rotating proxies ($50-200/mo) to avoid bans | One API subscription covers everything |
| **Multi-city** | Different subdomain per city, 700+ to manage | One endpoint, any city |
| **Maintenance** | Craigslist changes markup periodically | We handle all site updates |
| **Development time** | 1 week + ongoing proxy management | 5 minutes to first API call |

Craigslist has no public API and is one of the most aggressive platforms against scrapers. They block IPs, ban data centers, and require rotating residential proxies. This API eliminates all of that overhead.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
curl -G "https://craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com/craigslist/search" \
  -d "query=studio+apartment+sf" -d "limit=10" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
    "X-RapidAPI-Host": "craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com"
}

# Search for apartments
response = requests.get(
    "https://craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com/craigslist/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "studio apartment seattle", "limit": 10}
)
for item in response.json().get("results", []):
    print(f"{item['title']} - ${item['price']}/mo")
    print(f"  Location: {item['location']} | Posted: {item['postedDate']}")

# Search for deals on electronics
deals = requests.get(
    "https://craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com/craigslist/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "macbook pro los angeles", "limit": 10}
).json()
for item in deals.get("results", []):
    print(f"{item['title']} — ${item['price']} in {item['location']}")
```

### JavaScript

```javascript
  "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
  "X-RapidAPI-Host": "craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com"
};

const res = await fetch(
  "https://craigslist-classifieds-search.p.rapidapi.com/craigslist/search?query=furniture+nyc&limit=10",
  { headers }

const data = await res.json();
data.results.forEach(item => {
  console.log(`${item.title} — $${item.price} in ${item.location}`);
  console.log(`  Posted: ${item.postedDate} | Coords: ${item.latitude}, ${item.longitude}`);
```

offerup-local-marketplace.longDescription:
## OfferUp Local Marketplace API

Search OfferUp — the largest mobile-first local marketplace in the US with 20+ million monthly users. Find electronics, furniture, vehicles, clothing, and more listed by nearby sellers. Get structured JSON with pricing, location data, seller info, and photos.

### Features
- **Local listings**: Electronics, furniture, vehicles, clothing, home goods, sporting goods, tools, and 30+ categories
- **Location data**: City, state, GPS coordinates, and distance from search location
- **Item details**: Title, price, condition, description, photos, listing age, and direct URL
- **Seller info**: Username, rating, response time, number of listings, join date
- **Shipping options**: Local pickup and shipped item availability
- **Flexible search**: Keyword search with optional limit parameter

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/offerup/search` | Search OfferUp local listings by keyword |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Item name, category, or keywords
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "query": "macbook pro",
  "totalResults": 64,
  "results": [
    {
      "title": "MacBook Pro 14 M3 Pro 18GB 512GB - Like New",
      "price": 1400.00,
      "condition": "Like New",
      "location": "Seattle, WA",
      "latitude": 47.6062,
      "longitude": -122.3321,
      "seller": "TechDeals_SEA",
      "sellerRating": 4.8,
      "postedDate": "2026-03-28T14:30:00Z",
      "imageUrl": "https://images.offerup.com/...",
      "listingUrl": "https://offerup.com/item/...",
      "shipping": "Local Pickup"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Local deal finders**: Build apps that surface the best local deals within a radius of the user
- **Marketplace aggregators**: Combine OfferUp with Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace, and Mercari data
- **Flipping/arbitrage bots**: Identify underpriced items to resell on eBay or other platforms
- **Rental market research**: Track furniture and appliance pricing in specific metro areas
- **Vehicle sourcing tools**: Search for used cars, trucks, and motorcycles across local markets
- **Price comparison dashboards**: Show local vs online pricing for electronics and consumer goods

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | React Native web app, heavy JS rendering | Clean JSON with every field extracted |
| **Access** | No public API exists | Simple API key authentication |
| **Anti-bot** | Aggressive bot detection, device fingerprinting | No bot detection to bypass |
| **Mobile-first** | Optimized for mobile app, web scraping is difficult | Full web search data |
| **Maintenance** | Frequent app and site updates | We handle all changes |
| **Development time** | 2+ weeks for reliable scraper | 5 minutes to first API call |

OfferUp has no public API and is primarily a mobile-first platform. Their web interface has aggressive anti-bot measures and requires JavaScript rendering. This API handles all of that.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
curl -G "https://offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com/offerup/search" \
  -d "query=iphone+15" -d "limit=10" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
    "X-RapidAPI-Host": "offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com"
}

# Search for local deals
response = requests.get(
    "https://offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com/offerup/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "iphone 15 pro", "limit": 10}
)
for item in response.json().get("results", []):
    print(f"{item['title']} - ${item['price']} ({item['condition']})")
    print(f"  Location: {item['location']} | Seller: {item['seller']} ({item['sellerRating']})")

# Search for furniture
furniture = requests.get(
    "https://offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com/offerup/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "mid century modern desk", "limit": 10}
).json()
for item in furniture.get("results", []):
    print(f"{item['title']} — ${item['price']} in {item['location']}")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
  "X-RapidAPI-Host": "offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com"
};

const res = await fetch(
  "https://offerup-local-marketplace.p.rapidapi.com/offerup/search?query=ps5&limit=10",
  { headers }
);
const data = await res.json();
data.results.forEach(item => {
  console.log(`${item.title} — $${item.price} in ${item.location}`);
  console.log(`  Condition: ${item.condition} | Posted: ${item.postedDate}`);
});
```

### Pricing & Plans
The **free plan** (50 calls/month) lets you test local searches, validate the JSON schema, and prototype your marketplace tool. Upgrade to a paid plan for production-volume deal monitoring, local market analytics, and cross-marketplace aggregation workflows.

goodreads-book-data.longDescription:
## Goodreads Book Data API

Search Goodreads — the world's largest community for readers with 125 million members. Get book ratings, review counts, author info, genres, shelf data, and cover images as structured JSON. Browse by genre, search by title/author/ISBN.

### Features
- **Massive catalog**: Access data on 3.5 billion books cataloged on Goodreads
- **Community ratings**: Average rating (out of 5), total ratings count, review count — powered by 125M readers
- **Book metadata**: Title, author, publication year, ISBN, page count, genres, series info
- **Cover images**: High-resolution book cover image URLs
- **Genre browsing**: Browse top books by genre — fiction, mystery, sci-fi, romance, non-fiction, biography, and 100+ more
- **Direct links**: Goodreads URL for each book

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/goodreads/search` | Search books by title, author, ISBN, or genre |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Book title, author name, ISBN, or genre keyword
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "query": "project hail mary",
  "totalResults": 12,
  "results": [
    {
      "title": "Project Hail Mary",
      "author": "Andy Weir",
      "rating": 4.52,
      "ratingsCount": 1245678,
      "reviewsCount": 89432,
      "publishedYear": 2021,
      "isbn": "9780593135204",
      "pages": 476,
      "genres": ["Science Fiction", "Fiction", "Audiobook"],
      "imageUrl": "https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/...",
      "url": "https://goodreads.com/book/show/..."
    }
  ]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Book recommendation engines**: Build personalized reading suggestions using ratings and genre data
- **Reading list apps**: Create curated booklists with real-time rating data and cover art
- **Publishing market research**: Analyze genre trends, popular authors, and rating distributions
- **Library catalog enrichment**: Augment existing book databases with community ratings and reviews
- **Content generation**: Auto-generate "Top Books in X Genre" lists for newsletters or blogs
- **Bookstore pricing tools**: Cross-reference Goodreads data with pricing from Amazon, ThriftBooks, AbeBooks

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | Dynamic JS-rendered HTML | Clean JSON with every field extracted |
| **Access** | Goodreads retired their public API in 2020 | Full search and genre browsing |
| **Anti-bot** | Aggressive rate limiting and CAPTCHAs | Standard API rate limits |
| **Maintenance** | Site changes break scrapers | We handle all updates |
| **Development time** | 1-2 weeks for a reliable scraper | 5 minutes to first API call |
| **Data quality** | Parsing HTML is fragile and error-prone | Structured, validated JSON |

Goodreads officially retired their public API in December 2020 with no replacement. This API gives developers access to Goodreads community data that is otherwise unavailable programmatically.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
curl -G "https://goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com/goodreads/search" \
  -d "query=project+hail+mary" -d "limit=10" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
    "X-RapidAPI-Host": "goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com"
}

# Search by author
response = requests.get(
    "https://goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com/goodreads/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "brandon sanderson", "limit": 10}
)
for book in response.json().get("results", []):
    print(f"{book['title']} by {book['author']}")
    print(f"  Rating: {book['rating']}/5 ({book['ratingsCount']:,} ratings, {book['reviewsCount']:,} reviews)")

# Search by ISBN
isbn_result = requests.get(
    "https://goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com/goodreads/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "9780593135204", "limit": 1}
).json()
for book in isbn_result.get("results", []):
    print(f"{book['title']} — {book['rating']}/5 — {book['pages']} pages")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
  "X-RapidAPI-Host": "goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com"
};

// Browse sci-fi books
const res = await fetch(
  "https://goodreads-book-data.p.rapidapi.com/goodreads/search?query=science+fiction&limit=10",
  { headers }
);
const data = await res.json();
data.results.forEach(book => {
  console.log(`${book.title} by ${book.author} — ${book.rating}/5 (${book.ratingsCount} ratings)`);
  console.log(`  Genres: ${book.genres.join(", ")}`);
});
```

### Pricing & Plans
The **free plan** (50 calls/month) lets you test book searches, validate the JSON schema, and prototype your reading app. Upgrade to a paid plan for production-volume book lookups, catalog enrichment, and recommendation engine workflows.

thriftbooks-used-books-prices.longDescription:
## ThriftBooks Used Books & Prices API

Search ThriftBooks — one of the world's largest online used bookstores with 13 million+ titles. Find used and new books at discount prices with conditions ranging from Acceptable to Like New. Get structured JSON with pricing, ISBN, conditions, formats, and availability.

### Features
- **Massive inventory**: 13 million+ titles across fiction, non-fiction, textbooks, children's books, and rare finds
- **Condition grades**: Like New, Very Good, Good, Acceptable — with condition descriptions
- **Format options**: Paperback, hardcover, mass market, audiobook, e-book availability
- **Book metadata**: Title, author, ISBN-10, ISBN-13, publisher, publication year, page count
- **Pricing data**: Current price, original list price, discount percentage, and shipping info
- **Cover images**: Book cover image URLs and availability status

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/thriftbooks/search` | Search ThriftBooks listings by keyword |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Book title, author, ISBN, or keywords
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "query": "dune frank herbert",
  "totalResults": 24,
  "results": [
    {
      "title": "Dune",
      "author": "Frank Herbert",
      "price": 4.79,
      "originalPrice": 18.99,
      "condition": "Very Good",
      "format": "Paperback",
      "isbn": "978-0441013593",
      "publisher": "Ace",
      "year": 2005,
      "pages": 688,
      "imageUrl": "https://images.thriftbooks.com/...",
      "url": "https://thriftbooks.com/w/dune/...",
      "inStock": true
    }
  ]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Book price comparison**: Compare used book prices across ThriftBooks, AbeBooks, Amazon, and BetterWorldBooks
- **Reading list builders**: Create wishlists with buy links and show the cheapest available editions
- **Textbook sourcing**: Help students find the cheapest available textbook editions
- **Used book sourcing bots**: Find underpriced books for resale on Amazon or eBay (book arbitrage)
- **Library acquisition tools**: Source books for community libraries at the lowest possible cost
- **Publishing market analytics**: Track used book pricing trends by genre, author, or format

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | Server-rendered HTML with pagination | Clean JSON with every field extracted |
| **Auth** | No public API available | Simple API key authentication |
| **Anti-bot** | Rate limiting and bot detection | Standard API rate limits |
| **Maintenance** | Site layout changes break parsers | We handle all site updates |
| **Development time** | 3-5 days for a scraper | 5 minutes to first API call |
| **Data extraction** | Parse HTML tables and product cards | Flat JSON: title, price, ISBN, condition |

ThriftBooks has no public API. Their search results are server-rendered HTML with pagination that requires careful parsing. This API handles all of that and delivers clean, structured book data.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
curl -G "https://thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com/thriftbooks/search" \
  -d "query=dune+frank+herbert" -d "limit=10" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
    "X-RapidAPI-Host": "thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com"
}

# Search for cheap used books
response = requests.get(
    "https://thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com/thriftbooks/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "dune frank herbert", "limit": 10}
)
for book in response.json().get("results", []):
    discount = round((1 - book["price"] / book["originalPrice"]) * 100) if book.get("originalPrice") else 0
    print(f"{book['title']} by {book['author']} - ${book['price']} ({discount}% off)")
    print(f"  Condition: {book['condition']} | Format: {book['format']} | ISBN: {book['isbn']}")

# Search by ISBN
isbn_result = requests.get(
    "https://thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com/thriftbooks/search",
    headers=headers,
    params={"query": "978-0441013593", "limit": 5}
).json()
for book in isbn_result.get("results", []):
    in_stock = "In Stock" if book["inStock"] else "Out of Stock"
    print(f"{book['title']} — ${book['price']} ({book['condition']}) — {in_stock}")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
  "X-RapidAPI-Host": "thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com"
};

const res = await fetch(
  "https://thriftbooks-used-books-prices.p.rapidapi.com/thriftbooks/search?query=brandon+sanderson&limit=10",
  { headers }
);
const data = await res.json();
data.results.forEach(book => {
  console.log(`${book.title} by ${book.author} — $${book.price} (${book.condition})`);
  console.log(`  Format: ${book.format} | Pages: ${book.pages} | In Stock: ${book.inStock}`);
});
```

### Pricing & Plans
The **free plan** (50 calls/month) lets you test book searches, validate the JSON schema, and prototype your book pricing tool. Upgrade to a paid plan for production-volume price comparison, textbook sourcing, and book arbitrage workflows.

redfin-real-estate-property-data.longDescription:
## Redfin Real Estate Property Data API

Search Redfin — one of the largest real estate brokerages in the US covering 100+ markets. Get property listings with detailed data including price, beds, baths, square footage, year built, MLS numbers, price history, HOA fees, and GPS coordinates as structured JSON.

### Features
- **Property data**: Address, price, beds, baths, square footage, lot size, year built, property type (single family, condo, townhouse, multi-family)
- **MLS integration**: MLS number, listing agent, brokerage, days on market, listing status (active, pending, sold)
- **Price history**: Historical price changes, date of each change, sale history
- **Financial data**: HOA fees, tax assessment, estimated monthly payment, price per square foot
- **Location data**: Full address, GPS coordinates (latitude/longitude), neighborhood, school district
- **Listing status**: Active, pending, contingent, and recently sold properties

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/redfin/search` | Search Redfin property listings |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Address, city, ZIP code, or neighborhood
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "query": "Austin TX 78701",
  "totalResults": 47,
  "results": [
    {
      "address": "123 Rainey St #1205, Austin, TX 78701",
      "price": 685000,
      "beds": 2,
      "baths": 2,
      "sqft": 1150,
      "lotSize": null,
      "yearBuilt": 2019,
      "propertyType": "Condo",

      "mlsNumber": "ACT-2026-1234",

        {"date": "2026-03-18", "event": "Listed", "price": 685000},

      ],
      "listingAgent": "Jane Smith, Redfin",
```

### Use Cases
- **Investment analysis tools**: Evaluate rental yield, cap rate, and appreciation potential using property data
- **Property comparison apps**: Build side-by-side comparisons with beds, baths, price/sqft, and price history
- **Market trend dashboards**: Track median prices, days on market, and inventory across neighborhoods
- **Home valuation models**: Use price history, comparables, and property features for automated valuations
- **Real estate alert bots**: Notify investors when properties matching criteria hit the market
- **Relocation planning tools**: Compare housing costs, property types, and neighborhoods across cities

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | React SPA with server-side rendering and anti-bot | Clean JSON with every field extracted |

## Grailed Designer Fashion Marketplace API

Search Grailed — the leading marketplace for men's luxury and streetwear fashion. Access listings for designer labels like Rick Owens, Comme des Garcons, Balenciaga, and hype brands like Supreme, Stussy, and Palace. Get structured JSON with pricing, conditions, sizes, seller data, and photos.

- **Seller data**: Username, rating, feedback count, number of sold items, response time

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/grailed/search` | Search Grailed listings by keyword |

**Parameters:**
- `query` (required) — Designer name, item type, brand, or keywords
- `limit` (optional, default 25) — Results per page (max 50)

```json
}
```

### Use Cases

- **Fashion market analytics**: Analyze resale value trends for specific designers and categories

- **Collection management**: Track the resale value of a personal designer wardrobe
- **Drop monitoring**: Alert when specific items (e.g., Rick Owens Ramones) drop below target prices

### Why This API vs Direct Scraping

| | Direct Scraping | This API |
|---|---|---|
| **Format** | React SPA with infinite scroll, lazy-loaded images | Clean JSON with every field extracted |
| **Auth** | No public API available | Simple API key authentication |

Grailed has no public API. Their site is a React SPA with infinite scroll, lazy-loaded images, and CloudFlare protection. Building a reliable scraper requires headless browser infrastructure and constant maintenance.

graded-card-census.longDescription:
## Graded Card Census API

Access grading census data for professionally authenticated trading cards, including cards graded by PSA (Professional Sports Authenticator). Get population counts across all grade levels for single cards or entire sets.

> **Disclaimer**: This API is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by PSA or Collectors Universe. "PSA" is a registered trademark of Collectors Universe, Inc. and is used here solely for identification purposes.

### Endpoints
- `GET /psa/set?setName=Surging+Sparks` — Search by set name, returns all cards in the set
- `GET /psa/set?setUrl=<set-page-url>` — Direct set URL lookup
- `GET /psa/pop?certNumber=12345678` — Single card by certification number
- `GET /psa/pop?specID=12168502` — Single card by spec ID

### Features
- **Bulk set lookup**: Get population data for 100-500+ cards per request
- **Complete grade breakdown**: Auth, grades 1-10, half grades (1.5, 2.5...), qualifiers
- **Card details**: Number, name, variety (e.g. 'Special Illustration Rare'), year
- **Built-in caching**: Repeated lookups served instantly with 7-day freshness
- **Works for all categories**: Pokemon, sports cards, TCG, non-sport, coins, tickets

### Built For
Trading card investors analyzing set scarcity, eBay sellers adding census data to bulk listings, price guide sites building population databases, and collection tracking apps.

### Example Response
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "cached": false,
  "count": 397,
  "results": [{
    "specID": 12168745,
    "cardNumber": "240",
    "subject": "Hydreigon EX",
    "variety": "Special Illustration Rare",
    "setName": "2024 Pokemon Ssp EN-Surging Sparks",
    "year": "2024",
    "psaPop": { "total": 8849, "auth": 1, "grade9": 4610, "grade10": 3334 }
  }]
}
```

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
# Get census data for a full Pokemon set
curl -X GET 'https://graded-card-census-api.p.rapidapi.com/psa/set?setName=Surging+Sparks' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: graded-card-census-api.p.rapidapi.com'

# Look up a single card by cert number

  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: graded-card-census-api.p.rapidapi.com'
```

### Python
```python
import requests

url = 'https://graded-card-census-api.p.rapidapi.com/psa/set'
params = {'setName': '1986 Fleer Basketball'}
headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'graded-card-census-api.p.rapidapi.com'
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params)
data = response.json()
for card in data.get('results', []):
    psa10 = card['psaPop'].get('grade10', 0)
```

agent-memory.longDescription:
## Agent Memory Persistence API

Hosted persistent memory built for AI agents that need reliable, inspectable state across sessions.

### Workflow Outcome
Your LangChain agent needs to remember user preferences across sessions. Store `{"key": "user_prefs", "content": "prefers dark mode, UTC-5"}` in namespace `user-42`. Next session, search `/v1/memories/user-42/search?query=dark mode` — the agent picks up exactly where it left off. No Redis to manage, no database migrations. Ship persistent agent memory in 5 minutes.

### Features
- FTS5 full-text search ranked by relevance
- Batch store up to 100 memories per call
- Namespace isolation for multi-tenant agent systems
- Automatic TTL expiry for ephemeral state
- Metadata filtering via json_extract, access tracking
- Sort by recency or relevance

### Built For
Developers building LangChain/CrewAI workflows, MCP servers, chatbot context systems, multi-agent orchestrators, and any AI system needing durable state.

### Why Not Embeddings?
You control exactly what's stored and retrieved — no hidden embeddings, no surprise hallucinations. Inspectable, structured state that agents can trust.

### Example Use

**Free tier**: 50 requests/month. x402 USDC micropayments also supported.

```json
  }],

```

### Why This API?
No vector DB to manage, no embeddings to configure. Write a memory in one POST, retrieve it next session with one GET, search across all stored state with FTS5 full-text queries. Your agents get reliable, inspectable state in 5 minutes — not 5 days of infrastructure setup.

## Code Examples

### cURL


```python
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',

    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}

    'https://agent-memory.p.rapidapi.com/v1/memories',
    headers=headers,
    json={'key': 'user_prefs', 'content': 'Prefers dark mode, timezone UTC-5', 'namespace': 'agent-1'}
)
print(response.json())

# Full-text search across memories

    'https://agent-memory.p.rapidapi.com/v1/memories/agent-1/search',
    headers=headers,
```

agent-drift.name: Agent Drift Detection API
agent-drift.shortDescription: Monitor AI agent outputs for silent degradation. Golden test cases, 4 match modes, cron scheduling, webhook alerts, and a drift dashboard.
agent-drift.longDescription:
## Agent Drift Detection API

Monitor AI agent behavior and detect capability drift before it impacts users.

### Workflow Outcome
Your GPT-4 customer support agent starts hallucinating refund policies on a Tuesday. Without drift detection, you find out from angry users on Thursday. With this API: register a golden test ("What is our refund policy?" must contain "30-day"), schedule it every 6 hours, get a Slack webhook the moment the answer changes. Know in 6 hours, not 48.

### Features
- Register golden test cases with expected outputs
- 4 match modes: exact, json_subset, contains, status_code
- Cron-scheduled automated checks
- Webhook alerts when outputs deviate
- Run history with structured diffs
- Monitoring dashboard with drift rates and response times

### Built For
Developers running production AI systems, LLM-powered pipelines, and teams who need to know when their agents silently break.

### How It Works
1. Register a test: URL, expected output, match mode
2. Schedule via cron or trigger manually
3. Get webhook POST when drift is detected
4. Review structured diffs in run history

**Free tier**: 50 requests/month. x402 USDC micropayments also supported.

### Sample Response — Run Test
```json
{
  "testName": "weather-agent-check",
  "passed": false,
  "matchMode": "contains",
  "expected": "4",
  "actual": "I think the answer is four",
  "drift": true,
  "responseTime": 1234,
  "runAt": "2026-03-30T10:00:00Z"
}
```

### Why This API?
Uptime monitoring tells you an API is responding — drift detection tells you it's still returning correct answers. Register golden test cases, schedule cron checks, get webhook alerts when your agent's outputs change. Catch silent quality degradation before your users notice.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
# Create a golden test case
curl -X POST 'https://agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com/v1/tests' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name": "weather-agent-check", "url": "https://my-agent.example.com/ask", "method": "POST", "body": {"prompt": "What is 2+2?"}, "expected": "4", "matchMode": "contains"}'

# Run the test
curl -X POST 'https://agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com/v1/tests/weather-agent-check/run' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com'
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}

# Register a golden test
test = {
    'name': 'weather-agent-check',
    'url': 'https://my-agent.example.com/ask',
    'method': 'POST',
    'body': {'prompt': 'What is 2+2?'},
    'expected': '4',
    'matchMode': 'contains'
}
response = requests.post('https://agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com/v1/tests', headers=headers, json=test)
print(response.json())

# Run the test and check for drift
result = requests.post('https://agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com/v1/tests/weather-agent-check/run', headers=headers)
print(result.json())  # {"passed": true/false, "actual": "...", "diff": "..."}
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json'
};

// Register a golden test
fetch('https://agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com/v1/tests', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers,
  body: JSON.stringify({
    name: 'weather-agent-check',
    url: 'https://my-agent.example.com/ask',
    method: 'POST',
    body: { prompt: 'What is 2+2?' },
    expected: '4',
    matchMode: 'contains'
  })
}).then(res => res.json()).then(console.log);

// Run the test
fetch('https://agent-drift.p.rapidapi.com/v1/tests/weather-agent-check/run', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers
}).then(res => res.json()).then(console.log);
```

skillguard-premium.name: Skillguard Premium API
skillguard-premium.shortDescription: Verify AI agent skill safety before installation. Classifies skills as SAFE, CAUTION, or DANGER with confidence scores, audit trails, and batch verification.
skillguard-premium.longDescription:
## SkillGuard Premium API

AI agent skill safety verification — scan agent skills before deploying them in production.

### Features
- Classify skills as SAFE, CAUTION, or DANGER with confidence scores
- Detects: arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, credential exfiltration, reverse shells, cryptomining
- Flags caution-level: network access, file I/O, database queries, env var access
- Batch endpoint: verify up to 100 skills per call
- Permanent audit trail for compliance

### Endpoints
- `POST /verify` — single skill scan
- `POST /batch` — batch verification (up to 100)
- `GET /audit/:id` — retrieve audit record
- `GET /stats` — verification statistics

### Built For
AI infrastructure teams, MCP server operators, agent platform builders, and anyone deploying third-party skills in production.

### Zero Competition
Only agent skill safety API on any marketplace.

**Free tier**: 50 requests/month.

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
# Verify a single skill
curl -X POST 'https://skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com/verify' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name": "file_reader", "description": "Reads files from disk", "permissions": ["fs:read"]}'

# Batch verify multiple skills
curl -X POST 'https://skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com/batch' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"skills": [{"name": "web_search", "permissions": ["net:http"]}, {"name": "code_exec", "permissions": ["exec:shell"]}]}'
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}

# Verify a skill before installing
skill = {
    'name': 'database_query',
    'description': 'Executes SQL queries against user database',
    'permissions': ['db:read', 'db:write']
}
result = requests.post('https://skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com/verify', headers=headers, json=skill).json()
print(f"Rating: {result['rating']} (confidence: {result['confidence']})")
print(f"Flags: {', '.join(result.get('flags', []))}")
# Rating: CAUTION (confidence: 0.92)
# Flags: database_access, write_permission
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json'
};

// Single skill verification
fetch('https://skillguard-premium.p.rapidapi.com/verify', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers,
  body: JSON.stringify({
    name: 'shell_exec',
    description: 'Executes arbitrary shell commands',
    permissions: ['exec:shell', 'fs:write']
  })
}).then(res => res.json()).then(result => {
  console.log(`${result.name}: ${result.rating} (${result.confidence})`);
  // shell_exec: DANGER (0.98)
});
```

license-verify.longDescription:
## Professional License Verification API

One API call to verify contractor and nurse licenses across multiple US states. No need to navigate 6 different state board websites — get structured JSON with license status, expiration dates, bond info, classifications, and enforcement actions.

### Workflow Outcome
Your compliance team needs to verify a subcontractor before signing a $50K contract. One API call to `/contractors/search?state=CA&licenseNumber=1082017` returns the contractor's active/expired status, bond amount, workers comp carrier, and classifications — all in 200ms. No CAPTCHA, no manual lookup across 11 different state board websites.

### Why Use This Instead of State Websites?

Each state board has a different website, different search interface, and different data format. CA CSLB requires CAPTCHA. TX TDLR uses session-based forms. FL DBPR paginates results differently than NY. This API normalizes all of them into a single, consistent JSON response — one endpoint, any state.

| Pain Point | State Websites | This API |
|------------|---------------|----------|
| Format | HTML tables, PDFs, CAPTCHAs | Clean JSON |
| Integration | Manual scraping per state | One REST endpoint |
| Coverage | One state per site | CA, TX, FL, NY in one call |
| Maintenance | Sites change frequently | We handle updates |

### Contractor License Verification
- **States**: CA (CSLB), TX (TDLR), FL (DBPR), NYC (DOB/DCWP)
- Search by license number, person name, or business name
- Returns license status (active/expired/suspended), expiration date, classifications, bond info, workers comp, contact details

### Nurse License Verification
- **States**: FL (DOH MQA), NY (NYSED)
- Search by license number or name, filter by type (RN, LPN, NP, APRN, CNA)
- Returns license status, expiration, qualifications, enforcement actions

sec-insider-trades.name: SEC Insider Trading API
sec-insider-trades.shortDescription: Real-time SEC Form 4 insider trading data. Track stock purchases, sales, and option exercises by corporate officers, directors, and 10% owners from EDGAR.
sec-insider-trades.longDescription:
## SEC Insider Trading API

Real-time insider trading data from SEC EDGAR Form 4 filings. Track when corporate insiders — officers, directors, and 10%+ shareholders — buy, sell, or exercise options on their company's stock.

### Workflow Outcome
Build a Slack bot that alerts your trading desk when any S&P 500 insider buys 10,000+ shares. Query `/sec/insider-trades?days=1&type=P` every morning, filter for large purchases, post to Slack. Takes 20 minutes to build with the free tier. Upgrade to PRO when you're scanning daily across a 50-ticker watchlist.

### Why This API Instead of Free EDGAR?

The SEC EDGAR API is free and public — so why pay? Because raw EDGAR data is painful:

| | Free EDGAR | This API |
|-|-----------|----------|
| **Format** | Raw XML/XBRL that you parse yourself | Clean JSON, every field pre-extracted |
| **Rate limits** | 10 req/sec, no auth, frequent 503s | No EDGAR rate limits (we handle caching) |
| **Filtering** | Download full index files, grep for tickers | `?ticker=AAPL&days=30` — done |
| **Transaction parsing** | Nested XML with footnotes and amendments | Flat JSON: shares, price, type, value |
| **Bulk access** | Build your own index + parser + scheduler | One endpoint, paginated, real-time |

If you're building a trading dashboard, alert bot, or compliance tool, this API saves you 2-3 weeks of EDGAR parsing work.

### Features
- **Recent filings**: Get the latest Form 4 filings across all companies (200+ per day)
- **Company lookup**: Filter insider trades by ticker symbol (10,400+ supported)
- **Full transaction details**: Shares, price per share, transaction type, post-transaction holdings
- **Transaction types**: Purchases (P), Sales (S), Option Exercises (M), Gifts (G), Tax Withholding (F)
- **Insider info**: Officer title, director status, 10% owner flag

### Endpoints
1. `GET /sec/insider-trades` — Recent filings with pagination
   - `?days=7` — filings from last 7 days
   - `?ticker=TSLA` — filter by company
   - `?type=P` — purchases only
   - `?limit=50&offset=0` — pagination
2. `GET /sec/insider-trades/company/{ticker}` — All insider trades for a specific company
3. `GET /sec/insider-trades/filing/{accession}` — Full parsed Form 4 with every transaction

### Use Cases
- **Trading signals**: Track insider buying clusters as bullish signals
- **Compliance monitoring**: Monitor executive trading windows
- **Alert systems**: Build Slack/Discord bots for insider trade notifications
- **Financial dashboards**: Add insider activity feeds to trading platforms
- **Research**: Analyze insider sentiment across sectors or market caps

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "total": 234,
  "filings": [{
    "filedAt": "2026-03-30T16:30:00Z",
    "insiderName": "Jane Smith",
    "insiderTitle": "CFO",
    "ticker": "AAPL",
    "companyName": "Apple Inc.",
    "transactionType": "P",
    "shares": 5000,
    "pricePerShare": 178.50,
    "totalValue": 892500,
    "sharesOwnedAfter": 125000,
    "isDirector": false,
    "isOfficer": true
  }]
}
```

**Free tier**: 200 requests/month. Upgrade for higher volume.

## Code Examples

### cURL — Recent Insider Trades
```
curl -G "https://sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com/sec/insider-trades" \
  -d "days=3" -d "limit=10" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### cURL — Filter by Ticker + Transaction Type
```
curl -G "https://sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com/sec/insider-trades" \
  -d "ticker=TSLA" -d "type=P" -d "days=30" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "X-RapidAPI-Host: sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com"
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
    "X-RapidAPI-Host": "sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com"
}

# Recent insider purchases (bullish signal)
response = requests.get(
    "https://sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com/sec/insider-trades",
    headers=headers,
    params={"days": 7, "type": "P", "limit": 20}
)
for filing in response.json()["filings"]:
    print(f"{filing['ticker']} — {filing['insiderName']} bought {filing['shares']} shares @ ${filing['pricePerShare']}")

# All AAPL insider trades in last 30 days
aapl = requests.get(
    "https://sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com/sec/insider-trades/company/AAPL",
    headers=headers,
    params={"days": 30}
).json()
for f in aapl["filings"]:
    action = "BOUGHT" if f["transactionType"] == "P" else "SOLD"
    print(f"{f['insiderName']} ({f['insiderTitle']}) {action} {f['shares']} shares")
```

### JavaScript (fetch)
```javascript
const headers = {
  "X-RapidAPI-Key": "YOUR_KEY",
  "X-RapidAPI-Host": "sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com"
};

// Recent insider trades filtered by ticker
const res = await fetch(
  "https://sec-insider-trades.p.rapidapi.com/sec/insider-trades?ticker=NVDA&days=14&limit=10",
  { headers }
);
const data = await res.json();
console.log(`Found ${data.total} NVDA insider filings`);
data.filings.forEach(f =>
  console.log(`${f.filedAt} — ${f.insiderName} (${f.insiderTitle}): ${f.transactionType} ${f.shares} shares @ $${f.pricePerShare}`)
);
```

### Try It Free — Real SEC Data in 30 Seconds
Your free plan includes 50 requests. Try this right now:

```
GET /sec/insider-trades?ticker=AAPL&days=30&type=P
```

You'll get every Apple insider purchase from the last 30 days — names, titles, shares, price per share, total value — all as clean JSON. No XML parsing, no EDGAR rate limits, no CIK-to-ticker mapping. One call, done.

### Free vs Paid — When to Upgrade
| | Free (50/mo) | PRO $9.99/mo |
|---|---|---|
| Requests | 50 | 500 |
| Use case | Prototype alerts | Daily portfolio scans |
| Data | Full (all fields) | Full |

insider-buy-signal.name: Insider Buy Signal API
insider-buy-signal.shortDescription: Track when CEOs, CFOs, and directors BUY their own company stock — the strongest insider bullish signal. Filtered SEC Form 4 purchases as clean JSON. No XML parsing, no bulk downloads, no CIK lookups.
insider-buy-signal.longDescription:
## Insider Buy Signal API

Track when corporate insiders buy their own company's stock — widely regarded as one of the strongest bullish signals in the market.

### Why Insider Purchases Matter
Insider purchases are voluntary — executives spend their own money to buy shares because they believe the stock is undervalued. Studies consistently show that insider buying clusters precede above-market returns.

### Features
- **Purchases only**: Pre-filtered to show only buy transactions (type=P). No noise from routine option exercises, tax withholdings, or gifted shares
- **CEO/CFO/Director filtering**: Focus on C-suite and board member purchases — the highest-signal trades
- **Minimum value filter**: Find large-conviction buys ($100K+, $1M+) that signal serious insider confidence
- **Ticker lookup**: All insider purchases for any public company
- **Clean JSON**: Parsed from SEC EDGAR Form 4 XML — shares, price, total value, insider title, filing date

### Endpoints
- `GET /trades/search?type=P` — Search insider purchases with filters (ticker, role, min_value, date range)
- `GET /trades/ticker/{symbol}` — All insider purchases for a specific stock
- `GET /trades/recent` — Latest insider purchases across all companies
- `GET /stats` — Aggregate purchase statistics, top tickers, biggest buys

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "trades": [{
    "insiderName": "Jensen Huang",
    "insiderTitle": "CEO",
    "ticker": "NVDA",
    "companyName": "NVIDIA Corp",
    "transactionType": "P",
    "shares": 50000,
    "pricePerShare": 142.50,
    "totalValue": 7125000,
    "filedDate": "2026-03-28"
  }]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Trading signals**: Build buy-signal alerts for your portfolio watchlist
- **Investment research**: Screen for insider buying clusters by sector or market cap
- **Newsletter content**: Auto-generate "Top Insider Buys This Week" reports
- **Fintech dashboards**: Add insider purchase feeds to your trading platform

### Why This vs SEC EDGAR Directly?
| | Free EDGAR | This API |
|-|-----------|----------|
| Format | Raw XML/XBRL | Clean JSON |
| Filtering | All 100+ filing types mixed | Purchases only, pre-filtered |
| Parsing | 2-3 days of development | Ready to use |
| Rate limits | 10 req/sec, frequent 503s | No EDGAR rate limits |

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
# Get recent CEO/CFO purchases over $100K
curl -G 'https://insider-buy-signal.p.rapidapi.com/trades/search' \
  -d 'type=P&role=CEO&min_value=100000&days=30' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: insider-buy-signal.p.rapidapi.com'
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'insider-buy-signal.p.rapidapi.com'
}

# Find large insider purchases this week
response = requests.get(
    'https://insider-buy-signal.p.rapidapi.com/trades/search',
    headers=headers,
    params={'type': 'P', 'min_value': 500000, 'days': 7}
)
for trade in response.json().get('trades', []):
    print(f"{trade['ticker']} — {trade['insiderName']} ({trade['insiderTitle']}) bought ${trade['totalValue']:,.0f}")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'insider-buy-signal.p.rapidapi.com'
};

// NVDA insider purchases
fetch('https://insider-buy-signal.p.rapidapi.com/trades/ticker/NVDA', { headers })
  .then(res => res.json())
  .then(data => data.trades.forEach(t =>
    console.log(`${t.insiderName} bought ${t.shares} shares @ $${t.pricePerShare}`)
  ));
```

## Cross-Marketplace Price Tracker API

Search four major resale marketplaces from a single API — each with marketplace-specific depth.

### Marketplaces

### Why One API vs Four?
Each marketplace has its own search interface, auth flow, rate limits, and response format. TCGPlayer's API has a waitlist. Reverb requires OAuth. OfferUp has no public API. Poshmark blocks scrapers. This API handles all of that — normalized JSON from every marketplace.

```json
  }]
}
```

### Built For

- **Price comparison SaaS**: Build multi-marketplace search into your app
- **Deal-finding bots**: Alert when items drop below target prices

## Code Examples

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'cross-marketplace-price-tracker.p.rapidapi.com'

# Search Reverb for guitar deals

    'https://cross-marketplace-price-tracker.p.rapidapi.com/reverb/search',
```

### Try It Free — Compare Prices in 60 Seconds
Your free plan includes 50 requests. Try this:

You'll get current Reverb listings with prices, conditions, seller ratings, and make/model data — structured JSON, ready to use. Then try the same query on TCGPlayer for trading cards. Two marketplaces, one integration, normalized data.

| Requests | 50 | 500 |

Free tier: validate the response format, test your price comparison logic, confirm the data works for your use case. PRO: when you're running price monitoring in production — deal alerts, arbitrage bots, collection trackers, or marketplace analytics dashboards.

### Why Pay vs Building It Yourself?
Each marketplace has its own auth flow, rate limits, and response format. TCGPlayer's API has a waitlist. Reverb requires OAuth. OfferUp has no public API. Poshmark blocks scrapers. Building and maintaining 4 separate integrations takes weeks. This API gives you normalized JSON from all 4 marketplaces — one key, one format, one integration.

used-electronics-resale.name: Used Electronics Resale Price API
used-electronics-resale.shortDescription: Get resale prices for used phones, laptops, tablets, and electronics from Swappa and OfferUp. Two marketplaces, one API. Carrier, storage, battery health, local deals.
used-electronics-resale.longDescription:
## Used Electronics Resale Price API

Get resale prices for used phones, laptops, and electronics from two major marketplaces — Swappa and OfferUp.

### Marketplaces
- **Swappa**: Certified used electronics — carrier, storage, battery health, seller ratings
- **OfferUp**: Local marketplace — location-based deals, ZIP code filtering, radius search

### Features
- **Two marketplaces, one API**: Compare certified resale vs local deals
- **Device details**: Carrier, storage capacity, battery health (Swappa)
- **Location filtering**: Search by ZIP code and radius (OfferUp)
- **Sort options**: Price, newest, distance
- **Up to 50 results** per search per platform

### Endpoints
- `GET /swappa/search` — Certified used electronics with device details
- `GET /offerup/search` — Local electronics deals with location

### Sample Response (Swappa)
```json
{
  "results": [{
    "title": "iPhone 15 Pro Max 256GB",
    "price": 849.00,
    "condition": "Mint",
    "carrier": "Unlocked",
    "storage": "256GB",
    "batteryHealth": "98%",
    "sellerRating": 4.9
  }]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Phone buyback tools**: Price trade-in values against market
- **Trade-in calculators**: Compare certified vs local prices
- **Resale arbitrage**: Find underpriced devices on OfferUp to flip on Swappa
- **Price trackers**: Monitor device depreciation curves
- **Refurbishment businesses**: Source inventory at best prices

## Code Examples

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'used-electronics-resale.p.rapidapi.com'
}

# Swappa: certified prices
swappa = requests.get(
    'https://used-electronics-resale.p.rapidapi.com/swappa/search',
    headers=headers,
    params={'query': 'iPhone 15 Pro', 'carrier': 'Unlocked', 'sortBy': 'price_asc'}
).json()
for item in swappa.get('results', []):
    print(f"[Swappa] {item['title']} — ${item['price']} ({item['condition']}, Battery: {item.get('batteryHealth', 'N/A')})")

# OfferUp: local deals
offerup = requests.get(
    'https://used-electronics-resale.p.rapidapi.com/offerup/search',
    headers=headers,
    params={'query': 'iPhone 15 Pro', 'location': '90210', 'sortBy': 'price_asc'}
).json()
for item in offerup.get('results', []):
    print(f"[OfferUp] {item['title']} — ${item['price']} ({item.get('locationName', '')})")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'used-electronics-resale.p.rapidapi.com'
};

fetch('https://used-electronics-resale.p.rapidapi.com/swappa/search?query=MacBook+Air+M2&sortBy=price_asc', { headers })
  .then(res => res.json())
  .then(data => data.results.forEach(d =>
    console.log(`${d.title} — $${d.price} (${d.storage})`)
  ));
```
used-electronics-resale.seoTitle: Used Electronics Resale API — Phone, Laptop & Tablet Prices
used-electronics-resale.seoDescription: Get used electronics resale prices from Swappa and OfferUp. iPhone, MacBook, Galaxy prices. Carrier, storage, battery health. Trade-in valuation API.

rare-book-price.name: Rare & Used Book Price API
rare-book-price.shortDescription: Search ThriftBooks, AbeBooks, and Goodreads from one API. Used book prices, rare first editions, ISBN data, ratings, reviews, seller info. Three platforms, one integration.
rare-book-price.longDescription:
## Rare & Used Book Price API

Search three major book platforms from a single API — ThriftBooks, AbeBooks, and Goodreads.

### Platforms
- **ThriftBooks**: Used books at budget prices — condition, format, availability
- **AbeBooks**: Rare, collectible, and out-of-print books — ISBN, publisher, seller location
- **Goodreads**: Book ratings, reviews, and discovery — genre browsing, author info

### Features
- **Three platforms, one API**: Price comparison + discovery in one integration
- **ISBN and publisher data** (AbeBooks)
- **Condition grades** across platforms
- **Ratings and review counts** (Goodreads)
- **Seller details** and locations
- **Up to 50 results** per search per platform

### Endpoints
- `GET /thriftbooks/search` — Budget used book prices
- `GET /abebooks/search` — Rare and collectible book listings
- `GET /goodreads/search` — Book ratings and reviews by genre

### Sample Response (AbeBooks)
```json
{
  "results": [{
    "title": "The Old Man and the Sea",
    "author": "Ernest Hemingway",
    "isbn": "0684801221",
    "price": 1250.00,
    "condition": "First Edition, Very Good",
    "publisher": "Scribner",
    "datePublished": "1952",
    "sellerName": "Rare Books NYC",
    "sellerLocation": "New York, NY"
  }]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Book resellers**: Compare prices across ThriftBooks and AbeBooks for arbitrage
- **Library acquisition**: Find available copies with ISBN and condition data
- **Collection management**: Track values of rare book collections
- **Book recommendation apps**: Combine pricing with Goodreads ratings
- **Literary research**: Discover books by genre with review data

## Code Examples

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'rare-book-price.p.rapidapi.com'
}

# Search AbeBooks for rare first editions
rare = requests.get(
    'https://rare-book-price.p.rapidapi.com/abebooks/search',
    headers=headers,
    params={'query': 'first edition hemingway', 'limit': 10}
).json()
for book in rare.get('results', []):
    print(f"{book['title']} by {book['author']} — ${book['price']} ({book['condition']})")

# Compare with ThriftBooks prices
budget = requests.get(
    'https://rare-book-price.p.rapidapi.com/thriftbooks/search',
    headers=headers,
    params={'query': 'hemingway', 'limit': 10}
).json()
for book in budget.get('results', []):
    print(f"[ThriftBooks] {book['title']} — ${book['price']}")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'rare-book-price.p.rapidapi.com'
};

fetch('https://rare-book-price.p.rapidapi.com/goodreads/search?query=science-fiction&limit=20', { headers })
  .then(res => res.json())
  .then(data => data.results.forEach(b =>
    console.log(`${b.title} by ${b.author} — ${b.rating}/5 (${b.ratingCount} ratings)`)
  ));
```

agent-compliance-logger.name: AI Agent Compliance Logger API
agent-compliance-logger.shortDescription: Tamper-evident audit logging for AI agents. HMAC-SHA256 chained entries with cryptographic verification. Log decisions, actions, errors. GDPR redaction support. SOC 2 and regulatory compliance evidence.
agent-compliance-logger.longDescription:
## AI Agent Compliance Logger API

Tamper-evident audit logging for AI agent systems. Every entry is cryptographically chained — if any entry is modified, inserted, or deleted, the HMAC chain breaks.

### Why This API?
AI agents make autonomous decisions. Regulators, auditors, and enterprise customers need proof of what happened, when, and why. This API provides cryptographic chain-of-custody that's impossible to forge after the fact.

### Features
- **HMAC-SHA256 chaining**: Each entry links to the previous via cryptographic hash — tamper-evident by design
- **Chain verification**: One-click verification of entire audit history
- **Entry types**: memory, decision, action, error — structured logging for agent behaviors
- **GDPR redaction**: Soft-delete sensitive entries while preserving chain integrity
- **Agent + operator IDs**: Filter by specific agent or human operator
- **Chain export**: Full chain structure for offline verification or archival

### Endpoints
- `POST /v1/entries` — Append a new audit entry to the chain
- `GET /v1/entries` — List entries with optional agent_id filter
- `GET /v1/audit/verify` — Verify entire HMAC chain integrity
- `GET /v1/audit/chain` — Export full chain structure
- `POST /v1/entries/{id}/redact` — Redact entry for GDPR/PII compliance

### Sample Response (Append Entry)
```json
{
  "id": 42,
  "entry_sequence": 42,
  "entry_type": "decision",
  "agent_id": "trading-bot-v3",
  "entry_hash": "a1b2c3d4...",
  "entry_hmac": "e5f6g7h8...",
  "previous_hmac": "i9j0k1l2...",
  "timestamp": "2026-04-01T12:00:00Z"
}
```

### Sample Verification
```json
{
  "chain_intact": true,
  "total_entries": 1247,
  "last_verified_entry": 1247,
  "first_failure": null
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Enterprise AI compliance**: Prove agent behavior to auditors and regulators
- **SOC 2 evidence**: Cryptographic audit trails for compliance certifications
- **AI safety research**: Log and verify agent decision chains
- **Agent debugging**: Trace exact sequence of decisions and actions
- **GDPR compliance**: Redact PII while maintaining verifiable audit history
- **Incident response**: Replay agent behavior during post-mortems

## Code Examples

### cURL
```bash
# Log an agent decision
curl -X POST 'https://agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com/v1/entries' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com' \
  -d '{"agent_id": "trading-bot", "entry_type": "decision", "content": "Executed buy order for AAPL at $185.50"}'

# Verify chain integrity
curl 'https://agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com/v1/audit/verify' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Key: YOUR_KEY' \
  -H 'X-RapidAPI-Host: agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com'
```

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com'
}

# Log agent decisions
requests.post(
    'https://agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com/v1/entries',
    headers=headers,
    json={
        'agent_id': 'support-bot',
        'entry_type': 'action',
        'content': {'action': 'refund_issued', 'amount': 49.99, 'ticket': 'T-1234'}
    }
)

# Verify chain
result = requests.get(
    'https://agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com/v1/audit/verify',
    headers=headers
).json()
print(f"Chain intact: {result['chain_intact']} ({result['total_entries']} entries)")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json'
};

// Log and verify
await fetch('https://agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com/v1/entries', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers,
  body: JSON.stringify({ agent_id: 'my-agent', entry_type: 'decision', content: 'Approved loan application #5678' })
});

const verify = await fetch('https://agent-compliance-logger.p.rapidapi.com/v1/audit/verify', { headers });
console.log(await verify.json()); // { chain_intact: true, total_entries: 100 }
```
agent-compliance-logger.seoTitle: AI Agent Compliance Logger API — Tamper-Evident Audit Trails
agent-compliance-logger.seoDescription: Tamper-evident audit logging for AI agents. HMAC-SHA256 chained entries, chain verification, GDPR redaction. SOC 2 compliance evidence for enterprise AI systems.

resale-arbitrage-scanner.name: Resale Arbitrage Scanner API
resale-arbitrage-scanner.shortDescription: Search 5 resale marketplaces from one API — OfferUp, Poshmark, Grailed, Bonanza, Craigslist. Find underpriced items across platforms for resale arbitrage. Prices, conditions, locations, sellers.
resale-arbitrage-scanner.longDescription:
## Resale Arbitrage Scanner API

Search five resale marketplaces from a single API to find cross-platform arbitrage opportunities.

### Marketplaces
- **OfferUp**: Local deals — location filtering, ZIP code search, radius
- **Poshmark**: Fashion resale — brand filtering, sort by price/newest
- **Grailed**: Designer fashion — streetwear, sneakers, luxury
- **Bonanza**: General marketplace — vintage, collectibles, electronics
- **Craigslist**: Local classifieds — city filtering, category codes

### The Arbitrage Workflow
1. Search the same item across all 5 platforms
2. Compare prices to find underpriced listings
3. Buy low on one platform, sell high on another
4. Repeat at scale with automated monitoring

### Features
- **5 marketplaces, one API key**: No need to integrate 5 different data sources
- **Price filtering**: min/max price on all platforms
- **Location-aware**: ZIP code + radius on OfferUp, city on Craigslist
- **Sort options**: Price ascending to find cheapest first
- **Up to 50 results** per platform per search

### Endpoints
- `GET /offerup/search` — Local marketplace deals
- `GET /poshmark/search` — Fashion resale listings
- `GET /grailed/search` — Designer fashion listings
- `GET /bonanza/search` — General marketplace listings
- `GET /craigslist/search` — Local classifieds

### Sample Workflow
```json
// Same item, different prices across platforms:
{"platform": "OfferUp",    "item": "Nintendo Switch OLED", "price": 180}
{"platform": "Craigslist", "item": "Nintendo Switch OLED", "price": 195}
{"platform": "Bonanza",    "item": "Nintendo Switch OLED", "price": 275}
// Buy on OfferUp at $180, sell on Bonanza at $275 = $95 profit
```

### Use Cases
- **Resale arbitrage bots**: Automated cross-platform price comparison
- **Flipping tools**: Find underpriced items to resell at profit
- **Deal-finding alerts**: Monitor prices across 5 platforms simultaneously
- **Market research**: Compare pricing trends across marketplaces
- **Inventory sourcing**: Find best prices for resale inventory

## Code Examples

### Python
```python
import requests

headers = {
    'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
    'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'resale-arbitrage-scanner.p.rapidapi.com'
}

item = 'Nintendo Switch OLED'
platforms = [
    ('/offerup/search', {'query': item, 'location': '90210', 'sortBy': 'price_asc'}),
    ('/poshmark/search', {'query': item, 'sortBy': 'price_asc'}),
    ('/grailed/search', {'query': item}),
    ('/bonanza/search', {'query': item}),
    ('/craigslist/search', {'query': item, 'sortBy': 'price_asc'}),
]

for path, params in platforms:
    data = requests.get(
        f'https://resale-arbitrage-scanner.p.rapidapi.com{path}',
        headers=headers, params=params
    ).json()
    cheapest = data.get('results', [{}])[0]
    print(f"{path.split('/')[1]:12s} — ${cheapest.get('price', 'N/A')} — {cheapest.get('title', '')}")
```

### JavaScript
```javascript
const headers = {
  'X-RapidAPI-Key': 'YOUR_KEY',
  'X-RapidAPI-Host': 'resale-arbitrage-scanner.p.rapidapi.com'
};

const base = 'https://resale-arbitrage-scanner.p.rapidapi.com';
const results = await Promise.all([
  fetch(`${base}/offerup/search?query=AirPods+Pro&sortBy=price_asc`, { headers }),
  fetch(`${base}/poshmark/search?query=AirPods+Pro&sortBy=price_asc`, { headers }),
  fetch(`${base}/craigslist/search?query=AirPods+Pro&sortBy=price_asc`, { headers }),
]);
const data = await Promise.all(results.map(r => r.json()));
data.forEach(d => console.log(d.results?.[0]));
```
resale-arbitrage-scanner.seoTitle: Resale Arbitrage Scanner API — 5 Marketplaces, One API
resale-arbitrage-scanner.seoDescription: Search OfferUp, Poshmark, Grailed, Bonanza, and Craigslist from one API. Cross-platform price comparison for resale arbitrage. Find underpriced items to flip.

building-permits.longDescription:
## US Building Permits API — 8 Major Cities, 8.6M+ Records

Search building permit records across 8 major US cities with a single API.

### Supported Cities
- **New York City** — NYC DOB permits
- **Chicago** — Chicago building permits
- **San Francisco** — SF DBI permits
- **Los Angeles** — LA building permits
- **Austin** — Austin Development Services
- **Seattle** — Seattle SDCI permits
- **Dallas** — Dallas building permits
- **Philadelphia** — Philly L&I permits

### Features
- **8-city coverage** — 8.6M+ permit records from one API
- **Address lookup** — find permits by street address in any supported city
- **Permit details** — type, status, filing date, description, property info
- **City directory** — list all available cities and record counts
- **Statistics** — aggregate permit data by city
- **Official data** — sourced from city open data portals (Socrata + Carto)

### Endpoints
| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /permits/search` | Search permits by city + address |
| `GET /permits/details` | Get single permit details |
| `GET /permits/stats` | Aggregate statistics |
| `GET /permits/cities` | List supported cities + record counts |

### Sample Request
```
GET /permits/search?city=nyc&address=123+Main+St&limit=10
```

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "results": [
    {
      "permit_number": "123456789",
      "type": "New Building",
      "status": "Issued",
      "filing_date": "2025-01-15",
      "address": "123 Main St",
      "city": "nyc"
    }
  ],
  "count": 1
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Real estate investors** — check permit activity before buying property
- **Contractors** — find active construction projects and leads
- **PropTech apps** — add permit data to property listings
- **Urban analytics** — track development patterns across cities
- **Insurance underwriters** — assess property renovation history
- **Due diligence** — verify construction claims during transactions

fda-drug-recalls.name: FDA Drug Recall Alert API
fda-drug-recalls.shortDescription: Search FDA drug recall and enforcement data. Class I/II/III recalls, firm info, product descriptions, dates. Official openFDA data.
fda-drug-recalls.longDescription:
## FDA Drug Recall Alert API

Search FDA drug recall and enforcement actions using official openFDA data. Get structured recall data for pharmaceutical safety monitoring, compliance, and research.

### Why FDA Recall Data Matters
The FDA issues hundreds of drug recalls annually — from voluntary Class III withdrawals to life-threatening Class I recalls. Monitoring these is critical for pharmacies, hospitals, insurance companies, and compliance teams.

### Features
- **All recall classes**: Class I (dangerous), Class II (temporary/reversible), Class III (unlikely harmful)
- **Rich detail**: Firm name, city, state, product description, reason for recall, distribution pattern
- **Date filtering**: Search by recall initiation date or report date range
- **Status tracking**: Ongoing, Completed, Terminated recall statuses
- **Keyword search**: Search by drug name, firm name, or reason
- **Official source**: Direct from openFDA enforcement endpoint — authoritative government data

### Endpoints
| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /fda/recalls/search` | Search drug recalls by keyword, classification, date range, status |
| `GET /fda/recalls/recent` | Latest drug recalls across all classifications |
| `GET /fda/recalls/stats` | Aggregate recall statistics by class, year, state |

### Sample Response
```json
{
  "recalls": [{
    "recallNumber": "D-0123-2026",
    "classification": "Class I",
    "status": "Ongoing",
    "firmName": "PharmaCorp Inc",
    "city": "Indianapolis",
    "state": "IN",
    "productDescription": "Metformin HCl Extended-Release Tablets, 500mg",
    "reasonForRecall": "CGMP Deviations: impurity above specification limit",
    "recallInitiationDate": "2026-03-15",
    "distributionPattern": "Nationwide"
  }]
}
```

### Use Cases
- **Pharmacy systems**: Auto-pull recalled drugs from inventory
- **Hospital compliance**: Monitor Class I recalls affecting formulary
- **Insurance platforms**: Flag recalled medications in claims processing
- **Health apps**: Alert patients about recalled prescriptions
- **Regulatory research**: Track recall trends by firm, drug class, or region
- **News & media**: Auto-generate pharmaceutical recall reports

### Why This vs openFDA Directly?

|-|-----------|----------|
| Format | Nested JSON with 50+ fields | Clean, focused JSON |
| Filtering | Complex query syntax | Simple query params |
| Rate limits | 240 req/min with key, 40 without | Managed via RapidAPI |
| Aggregation | None — raw records only | Stats endpoint included |

## Code Examples
